channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Middle East War Triggers Higher Prices in China
item.description: Three and a half years of deflationary pressure on Chinese factories reversed course last month as higher energy prices cycled into the economy.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher

media:content.media:description: A car factory in Chongqing, China. Falling wholesale prices have forced thousands of manufacturers to sell their goods for less and less.

item.title: Picture Prompt: Brain Exercise
item.description: What do you think this image is communicating?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: How to Be Mayor in 100 Days: Mamdani Grapples With a Learning Curve
item.description: Zohran Mamdani has accrued a long list of quick accomplishments as mayor of New York City, but he has also abandoned some key campaign promises.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein and Sally Goldenberg

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani, a democratic socialist, has found it difficult to impose his ideals onto city government.

item.title: Mamdani’s First 100 Days as NYC Mayor: A Timeline
item.description: Zohran Mamdani has pushed to deliver tangible improvements to New Yorkers’ lives, amid larger challenges like a blizzard and a deficit-laden budget.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 10, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: How to Steal a Subway Train: First, Get Yourself a $10 Skeleton Key.
item.description: Inside the world of “conquesting,” where vandals break into the operator’s booth, fool around with the control panel — and maybe take a quick joyride.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Maslin Nir

item.title: Tracking 7 of Mamdani’s Biggest Campaign Promises
item.description: Zohran Mamdani based his successful campaign for mayor of New York City on several central pledges. Here’s an early look on where those stand.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos

item.title: Mayor Mamdani Answers Questions From New Yorkers
item.description: As Zohran Mamdani completes his first 100 days in office, we asked New York City residents what questions they have for their mayor.
item.dc:creator: Ang Li, Kassie Bracken and Mark Boyer

item.title: Oil Ticks Higher and Stocks Rise Ahead of U.S. Inflation Report
item.description: A temporary cease-fire in the war on Iran faces multiple hurdles and investors are watching the release of U.S. government data on inflation.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Las escritoras de novela erótica que eluden a los censores religiosos en Nigeria
item.description: Jóvenes mujeres obtienen ganancias de sus atrevidos textos que comparten a través de grupos de WhatsApp para esquivar a la policía moral.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Maclean, Ismail Auwal and Taiwo Aina

media:content.media:credit: Taiwo Aina for The New York Times

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘transaction’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 801 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: transaction
item.description: This word has appeared in 801 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Israel Strikes Hezbollah as Lebanon Impasse Threatens Cease-Fire
item.description: Israel said it would continue striking the Iran-backed militia. Iran said it would not attend peace talks with the United States in Pakistan if the truce was not extended to Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Salutes Melania’s White House Surprise
item.description: The first lady addressed her relationship with Jeffrey Epstein in a prepared statement that the talk show host called “better than her movie.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix

item.title: Después del alto al fuego, los iraníes viven una mezcla de alivio y conmoción
item.description: Los iraníes reflexionaron sobre lo que los ataques les habían hecho pasar y a lo que podrían enfrentarse a continuación.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi, Leily Nikounazar, Yeganeh Torbati and Shirin Hakim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Las películas de pesos pesados del cine dominan la programación de Cannes
item.description: La 79ª edición del festival incluye películas de directores de cine de autor tan reconocidos como Pedro Almodóvar y Pawel Pawlikowski, pero cuenta con pocos títulos de Hollywood.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall

media:content.media:credit: Christophe Simon/AFP — Getty Images; Patricia De Melo Moreira/AFP — Getty Images; Vianney Le Caer/Invision, vía AP
media:content.media:description: Las películas de Pawel Pawlikowski, Pedro Almodóvar e Hirokazu Kore-eda se encuentran entre las 21 que competirán por la Palma de Oro en el Festival de Cine de Cannes de este año.

item.title: Xi Seeks to Sway Taiwan, and Trump, With Message of Stability
item.description: Ahead of a summit with Donald Trump, the Chinese leader is using a rare meeting with a Taiwanese politician to cast Beijing as a peacemaker and squeeze the island’s president.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley

media:content.media:description: News coverage of the meeting between President Xi Jinping of China and Cheng Li-wun, the leader of the Nationalist Party in Taiwan, displayed at a restaurant in Taipei, on Friday.

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: Gaza’s Rubble Is the Grave of Its Future
item.description: Six month’s after the cease-fire, ordinary Gazans contemplate their future while living with the wounds of war.
item.dc:creator: Ghada Abdulfattah
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

item.title: For Putin, Iran Is Something Close to Irreplaceable
item.description: Vladimir Putin has spent years building a coalition of the discontented on the premise that authoritarian states can outlast Western pressure. Iran is his proof of concept.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Grajewski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: South African Writer Mfundi Vundla Gets Personal in His New Play
item.description: Mfundi Vundla spent 21 years in exile and created the popular television show “Generations.” His latest project is a play that explores the imperfections of the fight against apartheid.
item.dc:creator: Zimasa Matiwane

media:content.media:description: Mfundi Vundla, South African activist and writer, at the Johannesburg Theater after a performance of his new play, “Man With No Surname,” in March.

item.title: No Longer Off Limits, the Strait of Hormuz Remains Thorny Politically
item.description: Even after a cease-fire, Iran is keeping a chokehold on traffic, forcing countries to cut deals that could put them at odds with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A billboard that appeared over the weekend in Tehran shows Iranian soldiers with American military planes and ships caught in a net, with the message “The Strait of Hormuz will stay closed.”

item.title: Corrections: April 10, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Friday, April 10, 2026.

item.title: He Came to New York For a Job and Left With a Boyfriend
item.description: Shawn Miller didn’t get the job, but an unexpected connection with Ivan Gilkes on a dating app made the trip worthwhile.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Pilat

item.title: The Stranger Who Invited Me Into My Own Bed
item.description: It was more rom-com than horror movie.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Ortega

item.title: They Met at the Grammys and Eventually Hit the Right Note
item.description: It was music that bonded Ross Scarano, the editorial director of Complex Media, and Nina Lee, a publicist, and writing that reunited them.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: They Clicked Over Scrabble in the High School Cafeteria
item.description: Sarah Luciano and Samuel Bobley have “been crazy about each other since we were 16 years old,” when they met in Long Island, she said.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Block

item.title: Quote of the Day: Suez Signaled Britain’s Slide. Could Iran Do That for U.S.?
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, April 10, 2026.

item.title: How the U.S.-Israeli Strikes on Iran Have Damaged Schools and Hospitals
item.description: The Times has verified strikes on at least 39 schools and hospitals, a fraction of the devastation so far.
item.dc:creator: Leanne Abraham, Aurelien Breeden, Bora Erden, Anushka Patil, Christiaan Triebert, Daniel Wood and Karen Yourish
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: What’s Inside NASA’s Mission Control
item.description: Take a tour of the room where the team behind the Artemis II mission works day and night.
item.dc:creator: Marco Hernandez, Malika Khurana and Katrina Miller

item.title: ‘The Pitt’ Season 2, Episode 14 Recap: Physician, Heal Thyself
item.description: Dr. Robby may be stressed out by the constant demands of the E.R., but the work may also be the only thing saving him from himself.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins

media:content.media:description: Dr. Robby (Noah Wyle) took a moment to be alone with his thoughts. That might not be a good thing right now.

item.title: ‘Death of a Salesman,’ With Nathan Lane and Laurie Metcalf, Is Perfect for Our Time
item.description: Arthur Miller’s classic tragedy returns to Broadway, starring Nathan Lane and Laurie Metcalf. Yet again, it is a triumph.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

media:content.media:description: Laurie Metcalf and Nathan Lane in Joe Mantello’s revival of “Death of a Salesman” at the Winter Garden Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 10, 2026
item.description: Erica Hsiung Wojcik returns to open our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:description: Soak up the sparkling, grid-spanning entries in this Friday themeless.

item.title: White House Warned Staff Not to Engage in Insider Trading Amid War With Iran
item.description: The directive came amid a surge of suspiciously well-timed trades on oil and prediction markets just ahead of crucial moments in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Reporters working outside of the West Wing of the White House on Tuesday.

item.title: What to Know About the U.S. Military Draft Pool and Automatic Registration
item.description: For decades, draft-eligible men ages 18 to 25 have been required to register with the Selective Service System. Most states offer a registration option on driver’s license applications.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs (US)

media:content.media:description: U.S. Army soldiers during combat training in Honolulu, Hawaii, in 2024.

item.title: Patrick Radden Keefe on the Mystery at the Center of ‘London Falling’
item.description: The author discusses his newest book, about a 19-year-old’s curious death and the investigation that followed.

item.title: Erick Valencia Salazar, exsocio del Mencho, se declara culpable en EE. UU.
item.description: Cofundador del Cártel Jalisco Nueva Generación, Valencia Salazar se enfrenta a penas de entre 10 años y cadena perpetua tras llegar a un acuerdo de culpabilidad en un tribunal de Washington.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:description: Erick Valencia Salazar, conocido como el 85, en Ciudad de México en 2012.

item.title: Trump Attacks Tucker Carlson, Megyn Kelly Over Iran War Criticism
item.description: In a lengthy social media post, the president attacked Tucker Carlson, Megyn Kelly and others in starkly personal terms. He also criticized the editorial board of The Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump took aim at several conservatives in a long and often personal post on Truth Social, his social media platform, on Thursday.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Thursday
item.description: Israeli strikes against Hezbollah in Lebanon threatened the cease-fire between the United States and Iran ahead of scheduled peace talks.
item.dc:creator: Katie Reilly
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The aftermath of an airstrike in the Tallet el-Khayat area of Beirut, Lebanon, on Thursday.

item.title: Intelligence Court Renews Section 702 Surveillance Program
item.description: A classified ruling recertified the Section 702 program for a year but objected to systems that can filter Americans’ messages outside querying limits.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: Wiretapping and Other Eavesdropping Devices and Methods

media:content.media:description: Kash Patel, the F.B.I. director, attended a classified Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act briefing at the Capitol last month.

item.title: Unmasking the Creator of Bitcoin
item.description: The Daily’s Natalie Kitroeff and The New York Times Investigative Reporter John Carreyrou talk to Adam Back - the man Carreyrou believes is Bitcoin’s pseudonymous founder Satoshi Nakamoto.
item.dc:creator: The Daily Team

item.title: What We Know About the U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire Talks in Pakistan
item.description: Vice President JD Vance will lead the United States delegation which is set to meet with Iranian officials in Pakistan on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The Foreign Ministry offices in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Thursday. Pakistan will host talks between the United States and Iran.

item.title: Afrika Bambaataa, Often Called the ‘Godfather of Hip-Hop,’ Is Dead
item.description: A pioneering rapper and D.J. from the Bronx, Mr. Bambaataa was accused of child sexual abuse later in his career.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Abrams and Hannah Ziegler

media:content.media:description: Afrika Bambaataa performing in 2014 in Turin, Italy.

item.title: Judge Rejects Hegseth’s Second Attempt to Restrict Reporters at Pentagon
item.description: A federal judge gutted a set of rules that were adopted after the court declared an earlier press policy unconstitutional, in a case brought by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Erik Wemple
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: A federal judge in Washington said the Pentagon’s revised press policy was essentially unconstitutional.

item.title: How Trump’s Advisers Felt About Going to War With Iran
item.description: Our reporters Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan discuss how individual members of President Trump’s administration felt in the leadup to the war in Iran, and how they communicated their thoughts to Mr. Trump.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman, Jonathan Swan, Christina Shaman, John Pappas and Ray Whitehouse
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: A Mexican Cartel Leader and Ex-Partner to El Mencho Pleads Guilty in U.S.
item.description: Erick Valencia Salazar, a co-founder of one of Mexico’s deadliest gangs, faces 10 years to life in prison after making a plea deal in a Washington court.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:description: Erick Valencia-Salazar, known as El 85, in Mexico City in 2012.

item.title: Melania Says She Was Not a Victim of Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: Melania Trump said on Thursday that she was not a victim of Jeffrey Epstein, and that she had no knowledge of his crimes.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

media:content.media:description: Melania Trump, the first lady, made an unexpected statement about Jeffrey Epstein at the White House on Thursday.
